identity.name: Sui
identity.oneLiner: Sui is a layer 1 blockchain where everything is an object, programmed in Move with parallel execution.

sources.quote: Sui is a layer 1 blockchain that performs its own consensus and validation of transaction blocks.
Everything on the Sui blockchain is an object that has metadata, a type of ownership, and a referencing scheme.

entities.name: Sui
entities.description: A layer 1 blockchain that performs its own consensus and validation of transaction blocks.

sources.quote: Sui is a layer 1 blockchain that performs its own consensus and validation of transaction blocks.

entities.name: Object
entities.description: The fundamental unit of storage on Sui; every resource, asset, or piece of data onchain is an object.

sources.quote: An object is a fundamental unit of storage on the network. Every resource, asset, or piece of data onchain is an object.

entities.name: Move
entities.description: An open source language for writing safe packages to manipulate onchain objects.

sources.quote: Move is an open source language for writing safe packages to manipulate onchain objects

entities.name: SUI token
entities.description: The native token used as the currency of the network and to pay for gas fees.

sources.quote: the native SUI token as the currency of the network and to pay for the network's gas fees.

entities.name: Validators
entities.description: The entities that verify the blockchain's activity and lock SUI as collateral for an epoch.

sources.quote: the validator entities that verify this activity.

entities.name: Programmable Transaction Blocks (PTBs)
entities.description: Groups of commands that define all user transactions on Sui.

sources.quote: Programmable transaction blocks (PTBs) are groups of commands that define all user transactions on Sui.

entities.name: Shared and immutable objects
entities.description: Ownership categories of Sui objects; immutable objects cannot be mutated, transferred, or deleted.

sources.quote: Not owned by anyone, so anyone can use it. It cannot be mutated, transferred, or deleted

entities.name: Epochs
entities.description: Time periods on Sui during which the validator set remains unchanged.

sources.quote: Epochs define time periods on Sui where the validator set remains unchanged.

entities.name: Checkpoints
entities.description: Records that define the history of the blockchain so clients can verify their state.

sources.quote: define the history of the blockchain

entities.name: Storage fund
entities.description: A perpetual reserve funded by storage fees that pays out only the returns on its capital.

sources.quote: The storage fund pays out only the returns on its capital and does not distribute its principal

entities.name: Cryptographic agility
entities.description: Sui's support for multiple cryptography algorithms and primitives that it can switch between rapidly.

sources.quote: Cryptographic agility is core to Sui. The system supports multiple cryptography algorithms and primitives and can switch between them rapidly.

claims.text: PTBs let a user call multiple Move functions, manage their objects, and manage their coins in a single transaction without publishing a new Move package.

sources.quote: PTBs allow a user to call multiple Move functions, manage their objects, and manage their coins in a single transaction without publishing a new Move package.

sources.quote: if one transaction command fails, the entire block fails and no effects from the commands are applied

questions.question: What is Sui?
questions.answer: Sui is a layer 1 blockchain that performs its own consensus and validation of transaction blocks, using the native SUI token as the currency of the network and to pay gas fees.

sources.quote: Sui is a layer 1 blockchain that performs its own consensus and validation of transaction blocks.

questions.question: How does Sui store data with its object model?
questions.answer: An object is the fundamental unit of storage on the network; every resource, asset, or piece of data onchain is an object, and everything on Sui is an object with metadata, ownership, and a referencing scheme.

sources.quote: An object is a fundamental unit of storage on the network. Every resource, asset, or piece of data onchain is an object.

questions.question: What are Programmable Transaction Blocks (PTBs) on Sui?
questions.answer: PTBs are groups of commands that define all user transactions on Sui; a single PTB can perform up to 1,024 unique operations, executes commands in order, and applies all effects atomically.

sources.quote: up to 1,024 unique operations in a single execution

questions.question: How is Move on Sui different from Move on other blockchains?
questions.answer: Move on Sui addresses the scaling issue by not having global storage or its related operations, and it repurposes the address type as a 32 byte identifier used for both objects and accounts.

sources.quote: Move on Sui addresses the scaling issue by not having global storage or its related operations.

questions.question: How are gas fees paid on Sui?
questions.answer: SUI is the asset denomination needed to pay the gas fees required to execute transactions on the network.

sources.quote: SUI is the asset denomination needed to pay the gas fees required to execute transactions.

questions.question: What cryptography does Sui support?
questions.answer: Cryptographic agility is core to Sui; the system supports multiple cryptography algorithms and primitives and can switch between them rapidly, including verification within Move smart contracts through several signature schemes.

sources.quote: Sui supports verification within Move smart contracts through several signature schemes.

questions.question: What is the difference between owned, shared, and immutable objects?
questions.answer: An address-owned object is owned by a specific 32-byte address, an immutable object is not owned by anyone (anyone can use it but it cannot be mutated, transferred, or deleted), and a party/consensus object is owned by a single address but sequenced through consensus.

sources.quote: Not owned by anyone, so anyone can use it. It cannot be mutated, transferred, or deleted
Owned by a single address but sequenced through consensus

questions.question: What is the total supply of SUI and how does staking work?
questions.answer: The total SUI supply is capped at 10,000,000,000 SUI on Mainnet; SUI can be staked in the proof-of-stake mechanism, and validators lock SUI as collateral for the duration of an epoch and reward users based on the amount of SUI they delegate.

sources.quote: Provides the opportunity to stake SUI to participate in the proof-of-stake mechanism.
reward users based on the amount of SUI they delegate.

questions.question: What are epochs and checkpoints on Sui?
questions.answer: Epochs define time periods on Sui where the validator set remains unchanged, with reconfiguration adjusting network parameters at epoch boundaries, while checkpoints define the history of the blockchain.

sources.quote: Epochs define time periods on Sui where the validator set remains unchanged.
